problem.passage:
8. Read the passage in <A> and the conversation in <B>, and
follow the directions.【4 points】
<A>
Understanding second language (L2) learners’ interlanguage
is an important step for teaching L2 learners. In analyzing
interlanguage, it has been found that deviations from
characteristics of the target language exist in learners’
utterances. For example, deviations in early L2 learners’
utterances can be categorized into several types.
<Deviations in Early L2 Learners’ Utterances>
Example
(The intended meaning is Type Description
in parentheses.)
Mismatched The lexical class does not
It’s a pink. (It’s pink.)
lexical class match.
Semantic Utterances are semantically What’s the spaghetti?
deviation ill-formed. (Do you like spaghetti?)
Number of Utterances contain more or
I wore. (I wore a shirt.)
arguments fewer arguments than required.
I this book read.
Word order Word order is violated.
(I read this book.)
… … …
<B>
(Two students are carrying out a two-way spot-the-difference
task in their English class.)
S1: Now, let’s get started. In your picture, are there chairs?
S2: Yes.
S1: How many chairs are there?
S2: Two chairs.
S1: There are also two chairs in my picture. Now, please ask
me about my picture.
S2: What’s the pen?
S1: I’m sorry? Do you mean, “Do you have a pen?”
S2: Yes.
S1: Okay. Then, yes, I do. Do you have a pen?
S2: No, I do not have a pen.
S1: Okay. Then we’ve found one difference. Next, your turn.
S2: Is there a girl?
S1: Yes, there is. What is she doing?
S2: She is giving Mary.
S1: Um, what is she giving Mary?
S2: Ah, she is giving Mary a book.
S1: Oh, in my picture, she is giving Mary an eraser.
S2: Yeah! Finally, we got them all.
Note: S = student
Based on <A>, identify the TWO types of deviations found in the
students’ utterances in <B>. Then, explain your answers,
respectively, with evidence from <B>.
5
(8면 중 면)

_extraction.note: 자동 추출 초안. passage/meta/answer 모두 수동 보완 필요.
